title: Flow checking for propassign
body:
Flow checking for properties works differently than for hardware values. The big departures are due to how objects work:

1) An output property port cannot be read
2) An Input port on an object cannot be read (outside of the class body)
3) We can only assign the input ports of a local object declaration. A "remote" object (eg input object port, or output port of a local object) cannot be assigned into.
4) An output object port must be assigned at the root.
